_doc: Per-page data and slot requirements for the Relatedness Atlas. Round 1 (2026-05-11) ships labels + _doc only; requires_layers / requires_slots / preloads are out-of-scope for migrations per the kickoff rule (see 0_READ_ME_FIRST.md, 'Architectural-discipline rule (chat-38 step 17)'). Content decisions are owned by Quentin and resolved in a follow-up round once the ngsPedigree / inversion-karyotype data contracts settle.

network._label: network — PO / FS / ambiguous edge graph
network._doc: SVG force-directed layout of the active hub's pairwise edges. Edge classes: strong_po (black), possible_po (blue), ambiguous (amber, dashed), mendelian_conflict (red, dashed). Click a node to centre the evidence hub on that individual; the right-column Inspector recomputes against the clicked pair. Inline previews of the Karyotypes (top of the candidate list) and Inversions (paginated 4-row table) render below the SVG.

karyotypes._doc: Full karyotype matrix view: rows = individuals; columns = inversion candidates (subset chosen by chr filter). Cells colour-coded 0/0, 0/1, 1/1, NA. Ancestry stripe column shows the K=8 admixture decomposition for each row. STD/INV labels appear only when an inversion's named-allele convention is defined upstream.

inversions._doc: All inversion candidates from the Inversion Atlas (status, chromosome, start/end Mb, length, frequency, notes). Filters: status (PASS/WARN/FAIL), chromosome, frequency threshold. Click a row to scope the network panel and the inspector to that candidate.

mendelian._doc: Tests Mendelian segregation across selected dyads (parent x offspring, binomial test) or triads (parent_a x parent_b x offspring, multinomial / chi-square). Modes: single dyad, single triad, all dyads in hub, all triads in hub. Inversion subsets: all / PASS-only / WARN-only / freq >= 0.10 / current-chromosome-only. P-value thresholds: 0.05 / 0.01 / 0.001 / Bonferroni-corrected. Categories surfaced: STRONG_PASS, PASS, WARN, FAIL, TRANSMISSION_SKEW, DRIVE_CANDIDATE, NEEDS_CROSSES, FAMILY_SUSPECT.

compatibility._doc: Finds breeding partners that produce a target offspring karyotype at a chosen inversion candidate. Scopes: single candidate, all candidates on one chromosome (per-chrom mode), all candidates. Filters: sex-aware (requires opposite sex), exclude close kin (PO/FS), exclude ambiguous karyotypes. Useful for planning reciprocal crosses, fixing a haplotype, or producing diagnostic trios.

bdmi._doc: Per-candidate incompatibility screen. Test A: Mendelian segregation distortion across triads (or hub dyads). Test B: missing karyotype class (obs<5% when HWE exp>=15%). Test C: heterozygote excess/deficit relative to 2pq (|dev|>=30% or HWE chi-square p<0.05). Test D: ancestry x genotype contingency chi-square against dominant K-component. Test E: long-range forbidden combinations - minimum obs/exp ratio across cross-chromosome candidate pairs (<0.10 fires). Test F: phenotype association (stub until DEMO.phenotype or a normalized phenotype envelope is wired in). Confidence ladder: weak (none fire) -> moderate (B or C) -> strong (A or D or E) -> very strong (F) -> validated (out-of-scope for WGS, requires controlled crosses).

regimes._label: regimes — chromosome regime inheritance (linked inversions + mechanism classifier)
regimes._doc: Core-genetics control page for the BDMI screen. Section A: focal marginal Mendelian distortion (HWE chi-square). Section B: linked inversion regime control - for every other candidate on the same chromosome, computes a 3x3 joint genotype table, Cramer's V, mutual information, missing recombinant haplotype count, and the conditional-distortion combined p-value (Fisher's method across partner strata). Verdict: regime-coupled / regime-explained / regime-residual / regime-independent / regime-unknown. Section C: meiotic-drive vs underdominance classifier - aggregates triads in DEMO.triads by cross type (AA x AB, AB x BB, AB x AB) and asks whether the depletion follows the parent-transmitted allele (drive candidate) or the offspring AB class (underdominance candidate). Section D: breeding-risk interpretation, with the explicit hierarchy 'distortion -> linked-regime explained? -> drive vs underdominance -> BDMI'. Exports segregation_mechanism_classifier.tsv and linked_inversion_regimes.tsv.

meiosis._label: meiosis — crossover observables (single CO, double CO, coincidence, interference)
meiosis._doc: Parks the meiotic crossover model so it does not get lost. Section A: r1, r2, r12 inputs and computes C = r12 / (r1 * r2) and I = 1 - C. Section B: observable vs latent reference table - r1, r2, r12, C, I are observable / derived; a, b, d, x (precondition + exchange) are latent and not estimated from genotype data alone (3 equations, 4 unknowns - identifiability limitation). Section C: per-inversion-carrier CO frequency - stub until a per-individual CO call layer arrives (ngsPedigree Stage 3 chromosome inheritance map, or a cytological SPO11 / MLH1 layer). Section D: cross-page links to Regime inheritance (linked-inversion suppressed recombination) and BDMI (Test C heterokaryotype deficit, Test E long-range forbidden).
